title: Holiday Nature
description: Explore the botanical wonders of Christmas - from evergreen trees and holly to mistletoe and poinsettias, plus the natural symbolism of the season

kids_10_under.clue: This spiky green plant with red berries is often used to decorate homes at Christmas
These trees stay green all year round and are the most popular type of Christmas tree
This red and green plant is called the 'Christmas flower' and comes from Mexico
People kiss under this plant that hangs from doorways at Christmas
This type of tree, often used for Christmas, has needles instead of leaves
The red 'petals' on a poinsettia are actually special leaves called this
This evergreen tree is known for its wonderful smell and is named after a type of fur
Holly berries are this bright color that matches Santa's suit
Mistletoe is actually this type of plant that grows on other trees
This pine tree produces large cones and is named after a Scottish lake
The pointy leaves of holly are shaped this way to protect the plant from being eaten
This white substance on mistletoe berries is sticky and helps seeds stick to birds
This is the scientific name for evergreen trees that produce cones
In nature, holly berries are food for these animals during winter
The Aztecs called the poinsettia this name meaning 'flower that wilts'
Christmas trees have this shape that's wide at the bottom and pointy at the top
This green color represents eternal life at Christmas
Pine trees make this sticky substance that smells good
This type of fir tree is named after a U.S. state in the Northwest
Poinsettias need lots of this from the sun to stay healthy
Holly represents this season when it stays green while other plants don't
This is what we call the brown scaly things that grow on pine trees
Birds spread mistletoe by doing this to the berries
The star shape of holly leaves has this many points, usually
This is how many years it takes to grow a 6-foot Christmas tree
Mistletoe berries are this color, like tiny pearls
Only this gender of holly plant produces the red berries
This evergreen tree has soft, flat needles and is named after a country in Europe
The true flowers of a poinsettia are these tiny things in the center
Ancient people believed evergreens had this special power during winter
kids_10_under.correctQuestion: To ward off evil (or bring good luck)

kids_10_under.clue: Pinecones hold these tiny things that can grow into new trees
This is the country where poinsettias originally come from
Holly and ivy are both these because they stay green all year
This chemical in mistletoe berries makes them poisonous to eat
This is what the name 'evergreen' means about the trees
kids_10_under.correctQuestion: Always green (or never lose their needles)

kids_10_under.clue: Wreaths are often made from these bendable evergreen branches

metadata.theme: Christmas botanical traditions and natural science

difficulty_distribution.kids_10_under: Easy to medium, focusing on visual recognition and basic concepts
difficulty_distribution.teenagers: Medium to hard, incorporating scientific terminology and ecological concepts
difficulty_distribution.adults: Hard, featuring advanced botany, biochemistry, and specialized knowledge
